data.title: Max Resource announces IPO for Australian subsidiary
data.description:
• Max Resource (TSXV:MAX:CA) said that Max Iron Brazil, a majority owned subsidiary of Max intends to complete an IPO of a minimum of 30M shares in the capital of Max Brazil at a price of A$0.20 per share for minimum aggregate gross proceeds of AUD$6M up to a maximum of 50M shares for maximum aggregate proceeds of A$10M.
  • The net proceeds will be used, among other things, for the advancement of the Florália DSO Hematite Project located 67-km east of Belo Horizonte, Minas Gerais, Brazil, and for general working capital purposes.
  • Following the completion of the offering Max will continue to be a controlling shareholder in Max Brazil holding 88M shares and 12M performance shares.

data.title: Asian markets continue downward trend over Fed policy concerns
data.description:
Asian stocks continued the downward trend on Friday, after the Fed minutes suggested a potential slowdown in the pace of policy easing amid renewed inflation concerns.

The U.S. stock market remained closed for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

Japan’s benchmark Nikkei 225 (NKY:IND) traded 0.83% lower to 39,276.51, sliding for the third straight session and heading toward their second straight weekly drop.

Japanese household spending fell at a slower pace than expected, but the broader consumption trend remained soft, weighed by higher prices.

Japan’s foreign reserves declined by $8.28 billion to $1.12 trillion in December 2024, hitting the lowest level since July.

The index of coincident economic indicators in Japan, which includes data such as factory output, employment, and retail sales, declined to 115.3 in November.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index (HSI) edged 0.51% lower to 19,143.11, while the Shanghai Composite (SHCOMP) index lost 0.32% to 3,201.21.

China’s 10-year government bond yield rose to around 1.63%, following People's Bank of China's announcement to temporarily pause treasury bond purchases.

Industrial production in Malaysia expanded by 3.6% Y/Y in November and unemployment rate edged lower to 3.2%.

Retail sales in Indonesia grew by 0.9% Y/Y in November.

The Australian share market (AS51) traded 0.42% lower to 8,294.10, extending losses from the previous session, with financial, technology and healthcare stocks.

India's Sensex (SENSEX) retreated for the third straight session, down 0.17% to 77,480.47.

U.S. stock futures remained largely lower on Friday as investors prepared for the release of the December nonfarm payrolls report, which will provide key insights into the strength of the labor market: Dow -0.16%; S&P 500 -0.24%; Nasdaq -0.22%.

data.title: Firm Capital Mortgage Investment declares C$0.056 special dividend
data.description:
• Firm Capital Mortgage Investment (TSX:FC:CA) declares CAD 0.056 /share special year-end dividend.
  • The regular December 2024 dividend and the special year-end dividend total $0.134 per common share.
  • Payable Jan. 15; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See FC:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Non-farm payrolls

Non-farm payrolls track the number of part-time and full-time employees in both business and government. Economists expect 227K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.2%.

10:00 AM Consumer Sentiment

The University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index is composed of survey results comparing the relative level of both current and future economic conditions. There are two versions of this data released two weeks apart, preliminary and revised. The preliminary data tends to have a greater impact as it is more timely. The reading is compiled from a survey of around 500 consumers. The January preliminary print is expected at 74.5.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. The active rig count acts as a leading indicator of demand for oil products. Crude Oil Rigs in the United States edged lower to 482 on January 3 from 483 in the previous week.

data.title: Trump tariff fears causing disconnect in silver, copper markets - Bloomberg
data.description:
U.S. copper and silver futures are surging above rival international price benchmarks, as uncertainty and anxiety over the potential scope of the new Trump administration’s trade policies ramp up across financial markets, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

Front-month Comex silver traded at above an $0.80/oz premium over spot bullion prices set on the London Metal Exchange on Thursday, nearing a peak seen last month as traders reacted to Trump’s pledges to apply universal tariffs on all goods from all countries.

Front-month Comex copper also traded at a $623/ton premium over equivalent futures set in London, nearing record levels seen during last year's historic short squeeze that rocked the global copper market.

Traders have been rushing to ship copper into U.S. warehouses to cash in on the spike in prices since last year, and similar efforts have been underway since New York silver prices started to take off, Bloomberg reported.

Prices in the New York and London metals markets typically trade in near lockstep, and many algorithmic traders and hedge funds seek to make money with bets that any pricing gaps that do appear will close up quickly.

But investors can face huge losses if the price gap keeps widening, a dynamic that was a key factor behind last year’s copper squeeze, when arbitrage traders faced spiraling losses on their bets that Comex prices would fall relative to LME futures.

Some traders see a risk that history will repeat itself in the silver market, due to the limited availability of metal that can be readily delivered against Comex futures.

“The market is sleepwalking into a squeeze right now,” TD Securities senior commodity strategist Daniel Ghali told Bloomberg. at TD Securities.

Stockpiles in the London market have been drained following four years of severe shortfalls in global mined silver production, and further outflows risk creating a knock-on spike in prices, Ghali said.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ), (CPER), (COPX), (OTC:JJCTF)

data.title: Energy sector flips from S&P 500's 'worst to first' to start 2025
data.description:
Oil futures settled higher Thursday on signs of strong demand and prospects of higher heating fuel use, as cold weather gripped much of the U.S. and Europe.

Large swathes of the U.S. from east Texas to Virginia were under a winter storm warning, the National Weather Service said, lifting ultra-low sulfur diesel futures to three-month highs at nearly $2.38/gal.

Thursday's rise was "definitely winter fuel demand kicking in here in the U.S.," Again Capital's John Kilduff said.
J.P. Morgan analysts estimated that in the U.S., Europe and Japan, for every degree the temperature drops below its 10-year average, there is a 113K bbl/day increase in demand for heating oil and propane.
Meanwhile, the market structure in Brent futures indicates traders are becoming more concerned about tightening supplies at the same time demand is increasing.
The premium of the front-month Brent contract over the six-month contract reached its largest since August on Wednesday, a widening that typically indicates supply is declining or demand is increasing.

Oil futures retreated in the previous session from three-month highs in a selloff driven by large builds in U.S. product inventories.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for February delivery finished +0.8% to $73.92/bbl, and front-month March Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed +1% to $76.92/bbl, the seventh gain in nine sessions for both benchmarks.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) posted back-to-back gains, with forecasts for extended cold weather combining with production freeze-offs to tighten the market; the front-month February contract ended +1.4% at $3.701/MMBtu.

Also, front-month Nymex February ULSD (HO1:COM) closed +1.2% to $2.3782/gal, its best settlement since October 7.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Energy (NYSEARCA:XLE) tops the S&P 500's 11 stock sectors in the early days of 2025, shaking off two straight years when it lagged the market, with the S&P 500 Energy Index gaining 2.2% YTD and outpacing the overall 0.4% rise.

BTIG analyst Jonathan Krinsky said the energy sector has flipped from “worst to first” thanks to notable strength in natural gas stocks such as Antero Resources (AR), EQT (EQT) and Expand Energy (EXE), which are “showing clear leadership” as the commodity’s price bounces off multiyear lows.

But RBC Capital analysts led by Lori Calvasina downgraded the sector to Market Weight from Overweight, saying they “have the most pessimistic assessment for energy at the global sector level.”

data.title: Theratechnologies issues update on Egrifta SV shortage
data.description:
Theratechnologies (NASDAQ:THTX) said there will be a shortage of its drug Egrifta SV on the patient level in mid-January due to the voluntary shutdown of a contract manufacturing facility last year.

The Canadian company added that several U.S. pharmacies have reported stockouts of the drug.

Theratechnologies (NASDAQ:THTX) filed a Prior Approval Supplement with the FDA last month and has submitted questions about the status of two recently manufactured batches of the drug that are waiting to be released to pharmacies.

The FDA confirmed that it would respond to the company’s questions in writing no later than February 8, according to a company statement.

Egrifta SV is used to reduce excess abdominal fat in adults with HIV and lipodystrophy.

Theratechnologies (THTX) said in December that production of the drug had resumed following a voluntary shutdown of the contract manufacturing facility to address observations from an FDA inspection.

data.title: NOVONIX, Harper International sign licensing agreement for graphitization furnace technology
data.description:
• NOVONIX (NASDAQ:NVX) on Thursday said it has entered into a licensing agreement with Harper International Corporation, for the rights and use of its continuous, induction-based graphitization furnace technology.
  • Alongside this exclusive use agreement, the licensing agreement provides NOVONIX the right to an exclusive license to Harper’s technology, on which its continuous graphitization furnaces operate.
  • Upon making an initial payment within 12 months of the effective date of the agreement, NOVONIX will have the exclusive license to use the technology to further develop the furnaces used for the thermal production of graphite material for use in the battery anode market.
  • NOVONIX must make an initial payment no later than December 2025 to gain a license to design and develop the graphitization furnace technology for its internal use, said the company.
  • At the expiration of this seven-year period, the company will have a perpetual, irrevocable, and non-exclusive license to the technology for use only by NOVONIX or any sublicensee approved by Harper, it added.
  • NVNXF closed -8.0% at $0.46.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Pollard Banknote renews secured revolving credit facility
data.description:
• Pollard Banknote (TSX:PBL:CA) on Thursday said the company and its syndicate of lenders have executed a renewal of its secured revolving credit facility.
  • As per the renewal, the term has been extended by three years, maturing on December 31, 2028, said the company.
  • The facility size has been increased by $50 million to $291 million, with an accordion feature which can increase the facility by an additional $50 million, it added.
  • The company said the facility will be available for general working capital purposes, ongoing investments in capital expansion.
  • PBL:CA closed -1.05% at $24.27
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Canada Nickel Co extends repayment date of $15M loan facility
data.description:
• Canada Nickel Company (TSXV:CNC:CA) said on Thursday that it has extended the repayment date of the US$15 million loan facility announced July 9, 2024 with Auramet International, from January 9, 2025, to March 14, 2025.
  • An extension fee of US$438,465 will be paid by March 14, 2025 and 1,750,000 warrants with a strike price of $0.96 per share will be issued with a 9 month expiry. 750,000 warrants previously issued with the initial loan will be cancelled.
  • The loan will carry an interest rate of 1.25% per month for the extension period. The interest payable on the original loan amount has also been deferred to March 14th, 2025.
